items.question: For f(x, y) = x² + 2xy + y², what is ∇f(1, 2)?
Which direction gives steepest increase of f at x when ∇f(x) ≠ 0?
options: Any tangent to the level set
items.explanation: Among unit directions u, D_u f = ∇f·u is maximized when u aligns with ∇f.

items.question: In gradient descent on loss L, the standard update is:
items.explanation: Subtract η∇L to move opposite the steepest ascent direction of the loss.

items.question: If ∇f(x) = 0 at an interior point and f is differentiable, that point is:
options: A critical point (candidate extremum or saddle)
items.answer: A critical point (candidate extremum or saddle)
items.explanation: Zero gradient means no first-order ascent or descent; classify with second-order tests or context.
